rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions; avoid <error> types from unresolved references.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation annexes for such cases.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference undefined or inaccessible identifiers instead of inventing new ones.
generic_rules: Validate all AGREE expressions for type correctness and reference resolution before repair.
Use only declared or visible identifiers in AGREE contracts to prevent diagnostics.
Prefer component type contracts over implementation-level guarantees when possible.
Avoid introducing new package or data type references not present in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assignments in implementation scope must target valid output features or declared variables.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Guarantees in component type annexes should reference only visible ports and features.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by adding new declarations or imports.
Do not place implementation-level references in component type contracts.
Do not assume numeric or Boolean encodings for event ports without explicit model support.
Do not generate AGREE expressions with mismatched or unresolved types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
